metadata.title:
CARRY FIGHT


INTO PRIMARY

REPUBLICANS IN UPPER END
OF FIFTH WARD INSIST ON
HAVING REPRESENTATION
IN CITY COUNCIL.

pages.raw_text:
The election of Peter Nelson as
]. s. Phillips successor in the city
council has created some bad feel
ing among republicans in the upper
end of the Fifth ward. This feel
ing is not directed against Nelson
or anyone else in particular, but
the people in the upper end of the
ward think that they are entitled
to representation in the city coun-
Gil. The upper end of the ward
that is, the district embracing the
additions north of Euclid avenue
from Division street to Minnehaha
has never had a councilman, al
though it now comprises in area
the largest part of the ward, and
in population is rapidly gaining on
the lower portion of the ward.


The improvement clubs in the
Lidgerwood and Kenwood districts
had endorsed S. G. Wilson for
councilman and had backed up his
endorsement with a petition near
ly as large as that in favor of
Mr.. Nelson, yet Wilson's name was
not even presented to the council
for its consideration. One of the
hackers of Wilson served notice
on Councilman Ostrander that the
Lidgerwood and Kenwood people
expected to carry the fight into the
primaries in the spring and that
ii they could not get republican
candidate from the upper district
they would help elect democrat
to the city council Action by the
improvement clubs in regard to
the spring campaign will be taken
soon.
